method: Benford first-digit goodness-of-fit (chi-square df=8 + Nigrini MAD) on Linear A numeral magnitudes (v field). Magnitudes only; L2/L3; no sign values/readings.
key_findings: LA numeral magnitudes (n=1276, all v>=1, 0 excluded) are DEVIANT from Benford: MAD=0.0251 (Nigrini nonconformant) and chi-square GoF p~1.2e-19 (chi2=107.6, df=8).

The deviation is NOT uniform-random-like; it is a SUPER-Benford / heavy-small-magnitude pattern: leading 1s are OVER-represented (511/1276=40.0% vs Benford 30.1%) and high digits 7-9 are UNDER-represented (89/1276=7.0% vs Benford 18.0%).

Positive control PASSED cleanly: synthetic Benford classified CONSISTENT (MAD=0.0057, close), synthetic uniform classified DEVIANT (MAD=0.064, nonconformant), false-deviant rate on true-Benford = 0.0/20. The fitter is calibrated and discriminative.

Deviation is corpus-wide, not HT-only: Haghia Triada (n=834, MAD=0.0243), Zakros (n=126, MAD=0.0357), and Khania (n=121, MAD=0.0432) are all individually DEVIANT. Small sites (Arkhalkori, Tylissos, n<35) are too small to reject and read CONSISTENT by the OR rule.
Magnitudes span 1..3000 with a strong concentration at small values, consistent with administrative tallies of small lots rather than a single scale-invariant quantity.
successor_hypotheses: H1 (L2): Test whether the deviation is a MIXTURE-of-scales effect (Benford holds per commodity/transaction-type but the aggregate mixes scales) by partitioning numerals by preceding word/sign-cluster and re-fitting Benford per partition.
H2 (L2): Fit alternative first-digit models (e.g. truncated power law P(d)~d^(-k), log-normal magnitudes) and compare AIC; the super-Benford shape suggests a steeper power-law tail than Benford.
H3 (L2): Examine the magnitude RANK/FREQUENCY distribution (are magnitudes rounded? clustering at 10, 20, 100?) to test whether scribes used conventional round numbers, which would break Benford.

H4 (L3): Compare LA first-digit profile to published Benford analyses of Mycenaean Linear B accounting tablets (same script family, known language) to see if the deviation is script-specific or a general Aegean-administration signature.
H5 (L2): Restrict to magnitudes >= 10 (Benford is most reliable above small integers where rounding dominates) and re-test; small-integer dominance may be driving the super-Benford excess of 1s.
H6 (L3): Test second-digit and first-two-digit (FTD) Benford distributions for a finer-grained conformity check that is less sensitive to small-magnitude rounding.
non_circular: Magnitudes only (integer v field of num tokens). No sign assigned any phonetic/semantic value. No readings used. Pure structural first-digit statistics of the quantities. L2/L3 only.
